old_claim.note: No direct drying-and-salting edge was represented in the previous broad smoked-food claim.

new_claim.note: Cato's documented method requires twelve days packed in salt and two days drying in fresh air before the ham is smoked.

source_shape.source_locator: Cato, On Agriculture 162: packs hams in salt for twelve days and hangs them in fresh air for two days before the smoking stage.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Salting and air drying are explicit prerequisite stages in the attested combined cure.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The node is now locked to Cato's sequence, so these stages are constitutive rather than optional adjacent preservation methods.

ontology_before: The broad node explicitly kept drying and salting outside its prerequisite structure despite lacking direct evidence for smoking alone.
ontology_after: The source-pinned Roman method represents the actual combined salting, drying, and smoking sequence.
would_reject_if: Cato 162 were shown to describe smoking independently of its preceding salting and air-drying stages.
short_reason: The attested procedure salts and dries the ham before smoking it.
